title: The Investor's Manifesto
subtitle: Preparing for Prosperity, Armageddon, and Everything in Between
author: William J. Bernstein
authorBio: William J. Bernstein (b. 1948) is an American neurologist turned financial theorist and author. He earned a PhD in chemistry and an MD, practiced neurology in Oregon, and founded the website Efficient Frontier. He is the author of The Intelligent Asset Allocator and The Four Pillars of Investing, both considered classics of evidence-based personal finance. His writing combines historical narrative, quantitative rigor, and a deep skepticism of the financial industry.
keyConcepts: Risk and return are inextricably linked
Age = bond allocation rule of thumb
Value averaging vs dollar cost averaging
notableQuotes: The name of the game is not to get rich, but rather to avoid dying poor.
Nothing is more likely to make you poor than your emotions; nothing is more likely to save your finances than learning how to use cool, dispassionate reason to hold these emotions in check.
If you act on the assumption that every broker, insurance salesman, mutual fund salesperson, and financial advisor you encounter is a hardened criminal, you will do just fine.
In an environment filled with incredibly smart, hard-working, and well-informed participants, the smartest trading strategy is not to trade at all.
The goal is not to maximize the chances of getting rich, but rather to simultaneously allow for a comfortable retirement and to minimize the odds of dying poor.
An investor cannot earn high returns without occasionally bearing great loss. If the investor desires safety, then he or she is doomed to receive low returns.
Always think in after-inflation, or 'real' terms; this avoids having to correct later for the effect of long-term inflation.
You are not as good looking, as charming, or as good a driver as you think you are. The same goes for your investing abilities.
jonathanClementsForeword: A compact, brutally honest guide to building wealth over a lifetime — and surviving your own worst instincts along the way.
